ner: Grantham Institute at Imperial College London
summary: Ten years after the Paris Agreement, global temperatures have reached 1.45°C above pre-industrial levels, nearing the 1.5°C threshold.
Global CO₂ emissions remain high at 37.4 gigatons in 2023, despite renewable energy growth.
Major emitters like India and Brazil face criticism for insufficient climate action, with India's net-zero pledge seen as inadequate.
Fossil fuel companies spent $750 million lobbying against climate policies between 2020-2024.
The upcoming COP30 summit in Brazil is critical for updating climate commitments, with renewable energy growth offering some hope.
The Paris Agreement aimed to limit global warming to 1.5°C, but current emissions and temperature trends show progress is insufficient.
Global CO₂ emissions remain high, and countries must strengthen climate policies to meet targets.
The COP30 summit in Brazil in 2025 will be critical for global climate action.
Renewable energy growth offers hope, but urgent political will is needed to achieve ambitious climate goals.
